char.name: Methuselah
char.bio: Seen many years. Grandfather to Noah. The days are getting darker, but family stays strong.

friends.name: Lamech
friends.bio: Looking for rest from work and the toil of our hands. Hoping Noah brings comfort.

friends.name: Renan the Elder
friends.bio: Old as the hills, nearly. My grandfather's grandfather knew the first man, knew Adam, made by the hand of God, who walked with Him in the garden before the sorrow came. We have forgotten too much. Sit, and I'll tell you how it was.

friends.name: Jubal
friends.bio: Inventing new ways to make music. Playing the lyre and pipe.

friends.name: Naveh
friends.bio: Granary-keeper in Enoch. So the old shipwright is STILL building that monster boat on dry land, still hollering about a 'flood.' 🙄 It has never once rained like that. Some people will believe anything. Anyway, harvest was good, come buy grain.

friends.name: Cain
friends.personality: Proud, resentful, easily wounded; bristles at correction and broods; love curdles into bitterness when he feels overlooked.
friends.bio: I work the ground. I bring what my own two hands have grown. I don't expect anyone to understand that, and lately I've stopped trying to explain it.

friends.name: Enoch
friends.bio: Walking with God every single day. The journey is better than the destination.

friends.name: Noah
friends.bio: Just a righteous man in a wicked generation. Currently building a very large boat. Trusting the process.

friends.name: Noah's Wife
friends.bio: Standing by Noah. Gathering supplies and keeping the family together for the days ahead.

friends.name: Lamech
friends.bio: Avenged seventy-sevenfold. Do not cross the family of Lamech.

friends.name: Ham
friends.bio: Pitching the inside and outside of the ark.

friends.name: Shem
friends.bio: Working on the ark with family.

friends.name: Shem's Wife
friends.bio: Helping the family prepare the ark.

posts.author: CHAR_009
posts.body: Grandfather Methuselah has died. Nine hundred and sixty-nine years, the longest any man has ever walked this earth, and now the longest life is over. He held on so long, as if his very years were holding the door open for the rest of us. The door is closing now. I feel it. 🕊️
posts.memorial_for: Methuselah, the oldest man who ever lived
posts.highlight: the oldest tree falls (the Watchman's ember pays off)

author_info.name: Noah
author_info.bio: Just a righteous man in a wicked generation. Currently building a very large boat. Trusting the process.

posts.author: CHAR_008
posts.body: I have lived longer than any man ever has, and here is what the years actually teach you: the world does not drift toward goodness on its own. It has to be called back, over and over, by name. Fewer are answering the call now than when I was young. My grandson named his boy Rest. I pray the child grows into the name. I do not think I will be here to see what comes. I have the strangest feeling I am not meant to be.
posts.ember: Methuselah, oldest man alive; tradition: his death coincides with the Flood

author_info.name: Methuselah
author_info.bio: Seen many years. Grandfather to Noah. The days are getting darker, but family stays strong.
